_comment: Roadside nowhere-town pickup gigs (Step 53). Procedural middle-of-nowhere stops that fill the in-between nights you mark in the Tour Planner. Town names = an 'a' word + a 'b' word; venues are one-bar archetypes with a draw multiplier; reward scales off fame + venue draw. All data-driven so the builder can expand the pools and tune payout without code.
flavor: Population: not many. The bar's open, though.
No Walgreens, no signal, one stage. One shot.
The whole town turns out — all eleven of them.
Cheap beer, sticky floor, loud crowd. Perfect.
You're competing with the jukebox and the jukebox is winning. At first.
A wedding let out next door and wandered in.
Half the crowd is here for the wings. They stay for you.
The bartender's also the sound guy, the door guy, and the mayor.
